subject: UX & Research
title: The Aesthetic-Usability Effect
description: A fast breakdown of The Aesthetic-Usability Effect for builders.

hook.text: Masaaki Kurosu tested two ATM layouts in 1995. Same functions. Users rated the prettier one as easier to use.

buildup.text: The aesthetic-usability effect: attractive designs are perceived as more usable, even when they're not.

discovery.text: Apple mastered this. Sleek design makes users more patient with bugs and more forgiving of limitations.

twist.text: But beauty can mask real problems. A gorgeous app with broken navigation still frustrates — just more slowly.

climax.text: Beauty earns patience. But usability earns loyalty. The best products nail both.

punchline.text: Pretty buys patience. Usable earns loyalty. Do both.

quiz.question: What did Kurosu's ATM study reveal?
options: Users rated the more attractive design as easier to use, even with identical functions
Ugly designs are always harder to use
Visual design has no effect on perceived usability
